rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Good morning, what do you want ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: nimge eradu ruum mane saakaa ? innuu dodda mane beekaa ?
rows.english: Will a two room house do ? Do you want a still bigger one ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: nange erdu ruumu saaku, tumba dodda mane beeda.
rows.english: Two rooms are enough for me.   I don't want too big a house.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: Alla.  naavu naak jana, estu baadge ?
rows.english: No, we are four.   What is the rent ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Dosen't matter.   Don't require heavy advance.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Please come, sir.   What do you want ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I want vegetables.   What all do you have ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: ellaa ide.  badane kaayi, huruli kaayi, siime badane kaayi, don menisina kaayi ide.
rows.english: I have all.  brinjals, beans, khush khush, big chillies, etc.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Do you know the Government College ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes.   Do you have any work there ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Yes.   Do you know the Principal ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: alli nam hudugange ondu siitu beeku.
rows.english: I want a seat for my boy there.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: haagaadaree alli nange klaark obbru gottu.
rows.english: If so, I know a clerk there.
